title: blechnum novae-zelandiae
intro: Blechnum novae-zelandiae, also known as the karaka tree fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Blechnum novae-zelandiae include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the fronds of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
